name: Fresh Corn Ice Cream with Almond Lace Cookie
recipeInstructions:
1. Scrape 3 cups of fresh sweet corn from cobs.
2. Combine the corn and 1 cup of half and half in a blender or food processor, processing in short bursts to finely chop the corn.
3. Add the corn mixture to a saucepan with the remaining cream, corn cobs, and vanilla bean. Bring to a simmer over moderate heat.
4. In a separate bowl, beat together 8 egg yolks, 1/2 cup of sugar, 1/2 cup of light honey, 1 teaspoon lemon zest, and 1 teaspoon serrano chile until well combined.
5. Remove the cobs from the corn-cream mixture and whisk it into the egg yolk mixture in a slow, steady stream.
6. Remove the vanilla bean, split it, and scrape the seeds into the mixture. (Rinse the vanilla bean and use to flavor the sugar if desired).
7. Return the mixture to the saucepan and cook over low heat, stirring constantly, until it just begins to thicken (approximately 180°F). Be careful not to boil or the eggs will curdle.
8. Strain the mixture through a fine mesh sieve and chill before freezing in an ice cream freezer according to the manufacturer's directions.
9. Serve in chilled bowls with mint sprigs and almond lace cookies, if desired.
Almond Lace Cookies
1. Preheat the oven to 350°F.
2. Lightly butter and flour at least two baking sheets and place them in the refrigerator for at least 10 minutes.
3. In a large sauté pan, combine the remaining ingredients. Stir over low heat with a wooden spoon until the butter melts and the ingredients are well mixed.
4. Place heaping teaspoons of the batter about 5 inches apart on the prepared chilled sheets (the batter will spread during baking). Bake for 8 to 10 minutes or until evenly browned, turning the sheets halfway through baking.
5. Remove from the oven using a wide spatula and immediately shape as desired into tightly rolled cigarettes or allow to cool and serve flat.
6. Store in airtight containers for up to 2 days or in the freezer for up to a month.
